name: Risk, Compliance, and Financial Crime Subpack
description: Vertical-specialized value intelligence for Risk, Compliance, and Financial Crime functions in financial services. Covers AML/KYC, sanctions, fraud detection, credit/market/operational risk, model risk, regulatory reporting, SOX, consumer compliance, data governance, cyber risk, and third-party risk. ADDITIONAL components only; inherits base framework from master.

pains.name: Sanctions Screening Alert Overflow and Name Matching Errors
pains.description: Legacy name-matching algorithms generate excessive false positives on sanctions, PEP, and adverse media screening while missing true hits due to transliteration gaps and alias resolution failures.
symptoms: >95% false positive rate on sanctions alerts
Screening latency >500ms per transaction
Missed true positive hits in retroactive reviews
Manual remediation of >80% of alerts
OFAC/Sanctions regulator findings in last 24 months

pains.name: KYC Onboarding Friction and Periodic Review Backlog
pains.description: Manual KYC processes create 5-15 day onboarding delays, periodic review backlogs exceeding risk tolerance, and customer abandonment while regulatory expectations for beneficial ownership verification intensify.
symptoms: KYC onboarding cycle >10 business days
Periodic review backlog >20% of customer base
Customer abandonment during onboarding >25%
Beneficial ownership documentation gaps >30%
EDD case backlog >90 days

pains.name: Credit Model Performance Decay and Recalibration Delays
pains.description: Post-pandemic economic shifts causing PD/LGD model performance decay with Gini coefficients declining, while recalibration cycles extend beyond 12 months due to validation backlogs.
symptoms: Model Gini decline >5 points from development
Recalibration cycle >12 months
Model overrides >15% of decisions
CECL model sensitivity causing earnings volatility >$50M/qtr
Regulatory MRA on credit model soundness

pains.name: Market Risk VaR Backtesting Exceptions and Model Adequacy
pains.description: VaR models showing persistent backtesting exceptions (Kupiec test failures) with FRTB implementation creating capital inflation and methodology complexity.
symptoms: VaR backtesting exceptions >4 in 250 days
FRTB capital inflation >15% vs standard approach
Stress test result variance >20% between engines
Valuation model uncertainty (PRU) >5% of market risk RWA
Trading book/banking boundary disputes increasing

pains.name: Operational Risk Loss Event Under-Reporting and RCSAs
pains.description: Incomplete operational risk event capture, stale risk and control self-assessments (RCSA), and lack of forward-looking scenario analysis causing capital adequacy uncertainty.
symptoms: Loss event capture rate <60% of estimated actual
RCSA update cycle >18 months
Top 10 operational risks unchanged >2 years
Scenario analysis not linked to stress testing
Business line resistance to risk identification

pains.name: Fraud Detection Latency on Real-Time Payment Rails
pains.description: Legacy batch-based fraud detection unable to operate within FedNow/RTP sub-second decision windows, forcing transaction hold/friction or acceptance of fraud losses.
symptoms: Fraud detection latency >200ms on real-time rails
False negative rate on APP scams >40%
Transaction hold rate on RTP >5%
Customer complaint rate on held transactions >2%
Fraud losses on instant payments >0.50% of volume

pains.name: SOX ICFR Control Deficiency and Testing Inefficiency
pains.description: Manual SOX control testing consuming excessive internal audit and finance resources, with recurring deficiencies in IT general controls (ITGC) and user access reviews.
symptoms: SOX testing hours >15,000 annually
ITGC deficiencies >5 per year
User access review completion <80%
Management review controls with no system-enforced segregation
External audit fees increasing >10% annually due to scope expansion

pains.name: Consumer Compliance Complaint Spike and UDAAP Exposure
pains.description: CFPB/FCA complaint velocity increasing with UDAAP patterns emerging in product design, fee practices, and servicing; consent order risk elevating.
symptoms: Consumer complaints >150% of prior year
UDAAP-related complaints >30% of total
Fair lending disparities >3 standard deviations
Product change requests from compliance >20 per quarter
Regulatory inquiry or investigation active

pains.name: Cyber Risk Quantification and Board Reporting Gaps
pains.description: Inability to translate cyber risk into dollar-denominated exposure for board and risk committee reporting; FAIR methodology adoption stalled.
symptoms: Cyber risk reported in qualitative heat maps only
No dollar-denominated cyber exposure quantification
Board risk committee asking for cyber $ exposure
Cyber insurance coverage gap >$50M vs quantified exposure
Third-party cyber risk not included in enterprise cyber metrics

pains.name: Third-Party Concentration in Critical Risk Services
pains.description: Over-reliance on single cloud providers, payment processors, and SaaS vendors with inadequate contractual risk transfer and no real-time resilience monitoring.
symptoms: Single cloud provider >70% of critical workloads
No contractual right-to-audit on >50% of critical vendors
Vendor SOC 2 reports >12 months old for >20% of vendors
Concentration in top 3 vendors >60% of TPRM spend
No real-time vendor resilience score integration

pains.name: Regulatory Reporting Data Fragmentation and Reconciliation
pains.description: Multiple regulatory reporting pipelines (FFIEC, FINRA, ECB, PRA) with inconsistent data definitions causing restatements, manual reconciliation, and deadline compression.
symptoms: Data reconciliation between reports >5 business days
Same metric varies >3% between different regulatory reports
Manual data adjustments >10% of report fields
Late filing within 24h of deadline >20% of filings
Headcount dedicated to regulatory reporting >200 FTEs

pains.name: Model Inventory Sprawl and AI/ML Governance Gaps
pains.description: AI/ML model inventory growing >30% annually with inadequate documentation, monitoring, and validation coverage for non-traditional models.
symptoms: Total model inventory >1,000 with >30% AI/ML
Models without full documentation >25%
AI model monitoring frequency <quarterly
No unified model inventory across business lines
Model drift detected >6 months after occurrence

pains.name: Trade Surveillance Alert Fatigue and Market Abuse Detection
pains.description: Trade surveillance systems generating excessive noise on spoofing, layering, and cross-market manipulation while missing sophisticated algorithmic abuse patterns.
symptoms: Trade surveillance false positive rate >85%
Surveillance alert review backlog >14 days
No cross-asset surveillance coverage
Manipulation pattern detection lag >72 hours
Regulatory inquiry on market conduct >1 in 24 months

pains.name: Data Governance Lineage Gaps for Regulatory Remediation
pains.description: Inability to trace data lineage from regulatory report to source system causing prolonged remediation cycles, audit findings, and AI model distrust.
symptoms: Data lineage coverage <50% of critical reports
Regulatory data remediation cycle >45 days
Data quality exceptions in regulatory feeds >8%
AI model input data untraceable for >40% of fields
BCBS 239 gaps remaining >12 months after identification

pains.name: Operational Resilience and Critical Service Mapping Deficits
pains.description: Inability to identify critical business services and map impact tolerances to technology, people, and third-party dependencies; regulatory operational resilience deadlines approaching.
symptoms: No formal critical service register
Impact tolerance not defined for >60% of critical services
Recovery time objectives >regulatory expectations for >40% of services
No end-to-end scenario testing for critical services
Regulatory operational resilience deadline <18 months

pains.name: Anti-Bribery and Corruption (ABC) Program Gaps in Correspondent Banking
pains.description: Correspondent banking relationships with high-risk jurisdictions lacking adequate ABC monitoring, creating Wolfsberg Group non-compliance and de-risking pressure.
symptoms: Correspondent accounts in high-risk jurisdictions >15% of total
ABC transaction monitoring coverage <50% of correspondent volume
No automated PEP/sanctions screening on nested relationships
Wolfsberg Group questionnaire responses >30 days overdue
De-risking event or correspondent termination in last 12 months

pains.name: Climate Risk Stress Testing and Disclosure Readiness
pains.description: Emerging regulatory requirements for climate risk scenario analysis (NGFS, ECB, Fed) with inadequate data, methodologies, and governance frameworks.
symptoms: No climate risk scenario analysis in CCAR/ stress tests
Transition risk data coverage <30% of loan book
Physical risk exposure unquantified for >50% of CRE collateral
TCFD/ISSB disclosure gaps identified in audit
Regulatory climate risk deadline <24 months

pains.name: Internal Fraud and Insider Threat Detection Blind Spots
pains.description: Inadequate monitoring of employee trading, data exfiltration, and privileged access abuse with whistleblower reports increasing and cultural indicators ignored.
symptoms: Employee trading surveillance coverage <60% of restricted staff
Whistleblower reports increasing >25% YoY
No UEBA (User Entity Behavior Analytics) deployment
Privileged access review cycle >quarterly
Data exfiltration incidents with insider involvement >1 per year

kpis.name: Sanctions Alert False Positive Rate
kpis.formula: (False Positive Sanctions Alerts / Total Sanctions Alerts) * 100

kpis.name: Sanctions Screening Latency
kpis.formula: Average Milliseconds from Transaction Initiation to Screening Decision

kpis.name: Sanctions True Positive Detection Rate
kpis.formula: (True Positive Hits / Total Actual Violations) * 100

kpis.name: KYC Onboarding Cycle Time
kpis.formula: Average Business Days from Application to Account Activation

kpis.name: KYC Periodic Review Coverage
kpis.formula: (Customers with Current Periodic Review / Total Customers Requiring Review) * 100

kpis.name: EDD Case Backlog Age
kpis.formula: Average Days Open for Enhanced Due Diligence Cases

kpis.name: Credit Model Gini Coefficient
kpis.formula: 2 * AUC - 1 (where AUC is area under ROC curve)

kpis.name: Model Override Rate
kpis.formula: (Model-Overridden Decisions / Total Model-Driven Decisions) * 100

kpis.name: CECL Provision Volatility
kpis.formula: Standard Deviation of Quarterly CECL Provision / Average Quarterly CECL Provision

kpis.name: VaR Backtesting Exception Rate
kpis.formula: (Backtesting Exceptions in 250 Days / 250) * 100

kpis.name: FRTB Capital Multiplier
kpis.formula: (FRTB Standardized Capital / Current VaR-Based Capital) * 100

kpis.name: Stressed VaR Computation Time
kpis.formula: Hours to Complete Full Stressed VaR Run

kpis.name: Operational Loss Event Capture Rate
kpis.formula: (Reported Loss Events / Estimated Actual Loss Events) * 100

kpis.name: RCSA Currency Rate
kpis.formula: (RCSA Assessments Updated Within 12 Months / Total Active RCSAs) * 100

kpis.name: Scenario Analysis Coverage Ratio
kpis.formula: (Business Units with Current Scenario Analysis / Total Business Units) * 100

kpis.name: Real-Time Fraud Detection Latency
kpis.formula: Average Milliseconds from Transaction to Fraud Decision

kpis.name: APP Scam False Negative Rate
kpis.formula: (Undetected APP Scams / Total APP Scams) * 100

kpis.name: Instant Payment Fraud Loss Rate
kpis.formula: (Fraud Losses on Instant Payments / Instant Payment Volume) * 10000

kpis.name: SOX Testing Hours per Control
kpis.formula: Total SOX Testing Hours / Number of Active Controls

kpis.name: ITGC Deficiency Rate
kpis.formula: (ITGC Deficiencies / Total ITGC Controls Tested) * 100

kpis.name: User Access Review Completion Rate
kpis.formula: (Access Reviews Completed On-Time / Total Access Reviews Required) * 100

kpis.name: Consumer Complaint Velocity
kpis.formula: Complaints per 1,000 Accounts (or per $1B Revenue)

kpis.name: UDAAP Risk Indicator Rate
kpis.formula: (Products with Identified UDAAP Risk / Total Active Products) * 100

kpis.name: Fair Lending Disparity Index
kpis.formula: Statistical Measure of Pricing/Approval Disparity Across Protected Classes (e.g., Odds Ratio)

kpis.name: Cyber Risk Quantification Coverage
kpis.formula: (Critical Assets with Dollar-Denominated Risk Assessment / Total Critical Assets) * 100

valueDrivers.signalPattern: Sanctions false positive rate >90% with screening latency >300ms
Sanctions true positive miss rate >10% with OFAC findings in last 24 months
KYC onboarding >10 days with periodic review backlog >20%
EDD case backlog >90 days with beneficial ownership gaps >30%
Credit model Gini decline >5 points with override rate >15%
CECL provision volatility >$50M/qtr with earnings impact >5%
VaR backtesting exceptions >4 per 250 days with stressed VaR computation >8 hours
FRTB capital inflation >15% vs current approach with boundary disputes increasing
Operational loss event capture <60% with RCSA currency <70%
requiredEvidence: Loss event register vs insurance claims

valueDrivers.signalPattern: Scenario analysis coverage <60% with no linkage to capital planning
Real-time fraud detection latency >150ms with APP scam false negatives >30%
Instant payment fraud losses >10bps with reimbursement rate >40%
SOX testing hours per control >8 with ITGC deficiencies >5 annually
User access review completion <80% with manual control reliance >50%
Consumer complaints >150% YoY with UDAAP risk indicators >10% of products
Fair lending disparity index >1.2 with active regulatory inquiry
Cyber risk reported in heat maps only with no dollar quantification for >80% of critical assets
Third-party cyber risk coverage <50% with no real-time vendor monitoring
Critical vendor concentration >25% with due diligence currency <60%
No real-time vendor resilience monitoring for >70% of critical vendors
Regulatory report reconciliation >5 days with first-pass accuracy <80%
Cost per regulatory filing >$100K with headcount >200 FTEs
Model inventory coverage <80% with AI/ML validation coverage <50%
Model monitoring currency <70% with drift detected >6 months post-occurrence
Trade surveillance false positive rate >85% with manipulation detection lag >48 hours
Cross-market surveillance coverage <40% with no algorithmic abuse detection
Data lineage coverage <50% for critical reports with remediation cycle >45 days
Regulatory data exceptions >8% with AI model inputs untraceable >40%
No critical service register with impact tolerance undefined >60% of services
RTO achievement rate <70% with no end-to-end scenario testing for >50% of critical services
Correspondent accounts in high-risk jurisdictions >15% with ABC coverage <50%
Wolfsberg responses >30 days overdue with de-risking event in last 12 months
No climate risk scenario analysis with transition risk data <30% of loan book
TCFD/ISSB disclosure completeness <60% with regulatory deadline <24 months
Employee surveillance coverage <60% with whistleblower reports increasing >25%
Privileged access review <quarterly with insider data exfiltration >1 per year

formulas.name: Sanctions False Positive Reduction Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with 12+ months alert history; MEDIUM with vendor benchmark; LOW for new market entrants

formulas.name: KYC Onboarding Speed Revenue Impact
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with onboarding funnel data; MEDIUM with industry abandonment benchmark; LOW for B2B onboarding

formulas.name: Credit Model Performance Improvement Value
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with CECL model access and historical override data; MEDIUM using segment averages; LOW for new portfolios

formulas.name: Market Risk Capital Efficiency Improvement
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with FRTB parallel run data; MEDIUM using standardized approach comparison; LOW for non-FRTB jurisdictions

formulas.name: Operational Risk Capital Optimization
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with ORX membership data; MEDIUM using industry benchmark; LOW for firms with <50 reported events annually

formulas.name: Fraud Loss and Reimbursement Reduction
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with 24+ months fraud loss data; MEDIUM using Nilson/Javelin benchmark; LOW for new payment rails

formulas.name: SOX Compliance Cost and Audit Fee Reduction
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with current SOX cost breakdown; MEDIUM using Protiviti benchmark; LOW for first-year SOX firms

formulas.name: Consumer Compliance Cost Avoidance
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with complaint cost analysis and active inquiry data; MEDIUM using CFPB benchmark; LOW for firms with no active inquiries

formulas.name: Cyber Risk Quantification and Insurance Optimization
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with FAIR assessment and cyber insurance data; MEDIUM using IBM breach cost benchmark; LOW for zero-incident firms

formulas.name: Third-Party Risk Cost Avoidance and Efficiency
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with incident history and DD cost data; MEDIUM using Gartner benchmark; LOW for firms with <50 critical vendors

formulas.name: Regulatory Reporting Automation ROI
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with current state process mapping; MEDIUM using benchmark FTE ratios; LOW for newly regulated entities

formulas.name: Model Risk Management Efficiency and Deployment Acceleration
formulas.confidenceRules: HIGH with MRM function data and backlog analysis; MEDIUM using McKinsey benchmark; LOW for firms with <100 models

benchmarks.name: Credit Model Gini (Retail PD, Top Quartile)
APP Scam Reimbursement Rate (UK PSR Baseline)
benchmarks.source: UK Payment Systems Regulator APP Data

benchmarks.name: Cyber Risk Quantification Coverage (Target)
benchmarks.source: Gartner Cyber Risk Quantification Maturity Model

signalRules.name: Sanctions Screening Engine Age Rule
signalRules.signalPattern: Sanctions screening engine implementation date >5 years ago with no major upgrade
signalRules.confidenceRationale: Legacy name-matching algorithms degrade over time against evolving sanctions lists

signalRules.name: OFAC Consent Order Detection
signalRules.signalPattern: Active or settled OFAC consent order within last 36 months
signalRules.confidenceRationale: Regulatory enforcement is direct evidence of sanctions program weakness with quantifiable penalty history

signalRules.name: KYC Onboarding SLA Breach
signalRules.signalPattern: Customer onboarding SLA attainment <70% with abandonment rate >20%
signalRules.confidenceRationale: Directly observable operational metrics correlate to lost revenue and compliance risk

signalRules.name: CECL Model Validation Finding
signalRules.signalPattern: CECL model-related Matter Requiring Attention (MRA) from primary regulator
signalRules.confidenceRationale: Regulatory findings on CECL models directly indicate provision accuracy issues

signalRules.name: VaR Backtesting Exception Cluster
signalRules.signalPattern: 3+ backtesting exceptions in trailing 90 days with no model update in progress
signalRules.confidenceRationale: Basel framework explicitly ties backtesting exceptions to capital multiplier increases

signalRules.name: Operational Loss Reporting Inconsistency
signalRules.signalPattern: Operational loss events reported to ORX <50% of insured losses filed
signalRules.confidenceRationale: Insurance claim data provides independent validation but may include non-op risk categories

signalRules.name: FedNow/RTP Fraud Spike
signalRules.signalPattern: Instant payment fraud losses >2x non-instant payment fraud rate with transaction growth >30%
signalRules.confidenceRationale: Real-time rails inherently bypass batch detection; fraud rate differential is direct evidence

signalRules.name: APP Scam Liability Shift
signalRules.signalPattern: Regulatory mandate for APP scam reimbursement enacted or proposed in jurisdiction
signalRules.confidenceRationale: Regulatory liability shift creates immediate financial exposure on existing scam volume

signalRules.name: SOX Material Weakness Flag
signalRules.signalPattern: Material weakness or significant deficiency in ICFR disclosed in 10-K/A
signalRules.confidenceRationale: Public disclosure is definitive evidence of control failure with reputational and remediation cost

signalRules.name: CFPB Supervision Signal
signalRules.signalPattern: Active CFPB supervision or public enforcement action within 18 months
signalRules.confidenceRationale: Active supervision indicates validated consumer harm pattern with consent order precedent

signalRules.name: SEC Cybersecurity Disclosure Trigger
signalRules.signalPattern: 8-K Item 1.05 cybersecurity incident disclosure within 12 months
signalRules.confidenceRationale: Public disclosure mandates board-level attention and quantification requirements
signalRules.impactEstimateRange: $10M-$200M in incident cost and insurance impact

signalRules.name: Critical Vendor Single-Point-of-Failure
signalRules.signalPattern: Single cloud provider hosts >70% of critical workloads with no active secondary provider
signalRules.confidenceRationale: Architecture documentation and spend data provide direct, verifiable evidence

signalRules.name: Regulatory Filing Deadline Compression
signalRules.signalPattern: Late filing rate >15% with manual reconciliation between reports >5 days
signalRules.confidenceRationale: Filing timestamps and reconciliation schedules are directly auditable

signalRules.name: Model Inventory Uncontrolled Growth
signalRules.signalPattern: Model inventory growth >30% annually with validation backlog increasing
signalRules.confidenceRationale: Inventory growth without proportional validation capacity is a leading indicator of governance failure

signalRules.name: Operational Resilience Deadline Proximity
signalRules.signalPattern: Regulatory operational resilience deadline <18 months with no critical service register
signalRules.confidenceRationale: Regulatory deadline is immutable and non-compliance carries licensing implications

signalRules.name: Climate Risk Regulatory Mandate
signalRules.signalPattern: Regulator or exchange requiring climate risk disclosure with <24 months to compliance
signalRules.confidenceRationale: Regulatory direction is clear but specific requirements continue evolving

signalRules.name: Insider Trading Surveillance Gap
signalRules.signalPattern: Employee trading surveillance coverage <60% with whistleblower reports increasing >25%
signalRules.confidenceRationale: Coverage gap and complaint trend together suggest cultural and control risk

signalRules.name: Trade Surveillance False Positive Alert
signalRules.signalPattern: Trade surveillance false positive rate >85% with alert review backlog >14 days
signalRules.confidenceRationale: Directly measurable but alert classification requires domain expertise to validate

evidenceSources.name: UK PSR APP Scam Data
evidenceSources.coverage: UK APP scam and reimbursement data

buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Regulatory examination findings (MRA/MRIA) issued in AML, sanctions, or operational risk
buyingTriggers.timingImplication: 6-18 month remediation window with board accountability

buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Consent order or enforcement action from OFAC, FinCEN, CFPB, or state regulator
buyingTriggers.timingImplication: Immediate remediation mandate with independent monitor potential

buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Board risk committee requests dollar-denominated cyber risk exposure
buyingTriggers.timingImplication: Next board cycle (quarterly) with CRO/CISO pressure

buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: FRTB go-live date announced by national regulator
buyingTriggers.timingImplication: 12-24 months to implementation with capital impact at go-live

buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: APP scam reimbursement mandate enacted (e.g., UK PSR, US proposals)
buyingTriggers.timingImplication: 6-12 months to compliance with immediate liability on existing volume

buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: FedNow/RTP volume crosses threshold requiring real-time fraud detection
buyingTriggers.timingImplication: Immediate capability gap with transaction friction or fraud loss trade-off

buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: SOX material weakness disclosed or PCAOB inspection deficiency noted
buyingTriggers.timingImplication: Next annual filing cycle with remediation before restatement risk

buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Credit model Gini decline >5 points triggering validation backlog
buyingTriggers.timingImplication: Next model review cycle with regulatory notification potential

buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: AI/ML model deployment velocity exceeds validation capacity by >2x
buyingTriggers.timingImplication: Immediate governance gap with deployment moratorium risk

buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Operational resilience regulatory deadline <18 months with no service register
buyingTriggers.timingImplication: 18-month hard deadline with potential licensing condition

buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Major vendor outage or cyber incident creating board-level third-party risk attention
buyingTriggers.timingImplication: Immediate with quarterly board accountability cycle

buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Climate risk scenario analysis mandated by ECB, PRA, or Fed with disclosure deadline
Regulatory reporting late filing or restatement with supervisory escalation
buyingTriggers.timingImplication: Next filing cycle with potential enforcement escalation

buyingTriggers.triggerEvent: Internal whistleblower report volume spike >25% with active insider investigation

dislikedClaims: AI can fully replace human investigators without explainability
False positives are purely a cost issue, not a risk issue
Sanctions screening is just a checkbox compliance exercise

personas.roleTitle: Chief Model Risk Officer / Model Risk Manager
dislikedClaims: Black-box AI models are acceptable without interpretability
Model validation is just a formality before deployment
Regulatory guidance on MRM is vague and optional
Regulatory reporting is just a finance function
Manual reconciliation is acceptable for large banks
Data quality is purely an IT problem
Fraud is just the cost of doing business in payments
Rule-based systems are sufficient for modern fraud
Customer friction should never increase for fraud prevention
Vendor risk can be fully outsourced to procurement
SOC 2 reports are sufficient for all risk types
Third-party risk is only a cost center
Cyber risk cannot be quantified in dollar terms
Heat maps provide sufficient board-level insight
Cyber insurance eliminates the need for risk quantification

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current sanctions alert false positive rate, and what volume of alerts does your team disposition monthly?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Quantify current state pain and team capacity
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Alert volume, FP rate, investigator headcount, and cost per alert

discoveryQuestions.question: How many business days does your KYC onboarding process take from application to first transaction, and where do customers abandon?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Map onboarding friction and revenue impact
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Cycle time, abandonment rate, drop-off points, and CLV loss

discoveryQuestions.question: When did you last recalibrate your primary credit risk models, and how has the Gini coefficient trended over the past 18 months?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Assess model performance decay and regulatory risk
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Model age, Gini trend, override rate, and recalibration cycle

discoveryQuestions.question: How many VaR backtesting exceptions have you experienced in the last 250 trading days, and what is your current FRTB capital multiplier?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Quantify market risk model adequacy and capital impact
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Exception count, capital inflation, and boundary mapping status

discoveryQuestions.question: What percentage of operational losses do you estimate is actually captured in your event register versus what goes to insurance?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Capture rate, underreporting estimate, and RCSA currency

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your average fraud detection latency on FedNow/RTP transactions, and how does your APP scam false negative rate compare to industry?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Detection latency, APP scam rate, and reimbursement liability

discoveryQuestions.question: How many hours per control does your SOX program consume annually, and what is your ITGC deficiency rate?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Quantify SOX efficiency and control quality
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Testing hours, ITGC rate, automation level, and audit fee trend

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your current UDAAP risk assessment process, and how many products have been flagged in the last 12 months?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Assess consumer compliance maturity and exposure
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: UDAAP coverage, product flags, complaint velocity, and active inquiries

discoveryQuestions.question: How do you currently report cyber risk to your board risk committee—qualitative heat maps or dollar-denominated exposure?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Reporting format, FAIR adoption, insurance gap, and exposure value

discoveryQuestions.question: What percentage of your critical vendors have real-time resilience monitoring, and what is your vendor concentration ratio?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Monitoring coverage, concentration ratio, DD currency, and incident history

discoveryQuestions.question: How many days does it take to reconcile the same metric across your FFIEC, FINRA, and internal management reports?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Reconciliation time, variance rate, manual adjustment volume, and FTE allocation

discoveryQuestions.question: What percentage of your AI/ML models in production have been through your full validation process, and how do you monitor for drift?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Assess AI governance and monitoring maturity
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Validation coverage, drift detection frequency, and inventory completeness

discoveryQuestions.question: What is your trade surveillance false positive rate, and do you have cross-asset coverage across equities, fixed income, and derivatives?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: FP rate, cross-market coverage, alert aging, and market conduct history

discoveryQuestions.question: What percentage of fields in your critical regulatory reports have documented data lineage back to the source system?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Assess data governance maturity for regulatory remediation
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Lineage coverage, remediation cycle time, and BCBS 239 gap status

discoveryQuestions.question: Have you defined impact tolerances for your critical business services, and when was the last end-to-end scenario test?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Impact tolerance coverage, test frequency, RTO achievement, and regulatory deadline

discoveryQuestions.question: What percentage of your correspondent banking relationships have full nested customer screening coverage?
discoveryQuestions.discoveryIntent: Assess correspondent banking ABC program maturity
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Nested coverage rate, high-risk jurisdiction concentration, and Wolfsberg status

discoveryQuestions.question: What percentage of your loan portfolio has climate risk data, and have you run NGFS transition scenarios?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Data coverage, scenario analysis status, and TCFD/ISSB gap

discoveryQuestions.question: What percentage of your restricted staff are covered by automated trading surveillance, and what is your whistleblower resolution time?
discoveryQuestions.expectedInsight: Surveillance coverage, resolution time, UEBA deployment, and privileged access review status

objections.objectionText: We already have a sanctions screening vendor and don't want to replace it.
objections.responseStrategy: Position as augmentation/tuning layer rather than replacement. Highlight that 90%+ FP rates indicate the engine needs AI tuning overlay, not replacement. Reference case study where tuning layer reduced FP 40% without engine swap.
counterEvidence: Deloitte Sanctions Survey 2024: 70% of institutions augment rather than replace
Nice Actimize case study: Tuning overlay reduced FP 35% on legacy engine
objections.bestPractice: Lead with tuning ROI and risk of missing true positives, not vendor replacement

objections.objectionText: Our regulators prefer simple, explainable models—we can't use AI/ML.
objections.responseStrategy: Distinguish between black-box and explainable AI. Reference SR 11-7 and OCC guidance on model risk—regulators approve AI with adequate documentation, monitoring, and validation. Provide validated AI model governance framework example.
counterEvidence: Federal Reserve SR 11-7: Model risk management applies to all models regardless of technique
OCC guidance explicitly allows ML with appropriate governance
McKinsey: 60% of top-quartile banks use ML in credit models with regulatory approval
objections.bestPractice: Frame as 'governed AI' not 'black-box AI'; emphasize documentation and monitoring controls

objections.objectionText: Regulatory reporting is a cost center—we can't justify significant investment.
objections.responseStrategy: Reframe as enterprise data governance investment with regulatory reporting as one use case. Quantify restatement risk, late filing penalties, and manual reconciliation labor. Demonstrate how single data pipeline serves compliance, risk, and finance.
counterEvidence: Accenture study: Regulatory reporting automation reduces total cost 40% while improving accuracy
Average SEC late filing penalty: $1.5M; restatement cost: $5M-$20M
objections.bestPractice: Use broader data governance business case; regulatory reporting is the compelling event, not the sole ROI

objections.objectionText: Our fraud losses are within our budget—we don't need new detection tools.
objections.responseStrategy: Shift from loss budget to total cost of fraud including customer complaints, reputational damage, regulatory reimbursement mandates, and customer churn. Calculate reimbursement liability under emerging mandates.
counterEvidence: UK PSR: Average APP scam reimbursement now 58% and rising
Javelin: True cost of fraud is 3-5x direct losses when including remediation and churn
objections.bestPractice: Calculate total cost of fraud including regulatory, reputational, and customer lifetime value impacts

objections.objectionText: Cyber risk can't be quantified—it's too dynamic and uncertain.
objections.responseStrategy: Reference FAIR methodology adoption by 40%+ Fortune 500 companies. Quantify with scenario-based ranges rather than point estimates. Board committees increasingly expect dollar exposure; absence is a governance gap, not a sophistication advantage.
counterEvidence: FAIR Institute: 450+ member organizations using quantitative cyber risk
SEC disclosure rules now require material cyber incident cost quantification
Gartner: 70% of boards will require cyber risk quantification by 2026
objections.bestPractice: Start with scenario-based ranges, not single-point estimates; build credibility incrementally

objections.objectionText: Our third-party risk program is mature—we have questionnaires and SOC 2 reviews.
objections.responseStrategy: Acknowledge maturity but highlight concentration risk and real-time resilience gaps. Point to recent major vendor outages that had SOC 2 reports but no resilience monitoring. Quantify revenue-at-risk from single-vendor failure.
counterEvidence: Gartner: 60% of organizations with SOC 2 reviews experienced vendor-related incidents
CrowdStrike outage July 2024: 8.5M Windows devices affected despite SOC 2 compliance
OCC Bulletin 2013-29: Due diligence is continuous, not point-in-time
objections.bestPractice: Use recent vendor outage examples to create urgency; SOC 2 is necessary but not sufficient

objections.objectionText: We can't change anything during a regulatory examination or remediation period.
objections.responseStrategy: Position change as supporting the remediation, not competing with it. Regulators expect sustainable solutions, not temporary fixes. Show how technology enables examiner confidence through audit trails and documentation.
counterEvidence: OCC: Technology-enabled compliance is viewed favorably in MRA remediation
Multiple consent orders explicitly require 'sustainable technology solutions'
objections.bestPractice: Frame as enabling faster, more defensible remediation; get regulator buy-in through examiner outreach

objections.objectionText: Our model risk team is small—we don't have capacity to validate new AI models.
objections.responseStrategy: Offer automated validation tools that reduce per-model validation effort by 50-70%. Frame as capacity multiplier, not capacity requirement. Reference MRM platform implementations that enabled 3x model growth without headcount increase.
counterEvidence: McKinsey MRM Survey: Automated validation tools reduce per-model effort 60%
Model validation platform case study: 200% model growth with 10% headcount increase
objections.bestPractice: Lead with capacity multiplier value proposition, not headcount requirements

objections.objectionText: We've already invested heavily in our GRC platform—why add another system?
objections.responseStrategy: Position as integration with existing GRC, not replacement. Highlight that most GRC platforms lack specialized modules for cyber risk quantification, real-time vendor monitoring, or AI model governance. Focus on filling specific gaps.
counterEvidence: Gartner: 70% of enterprises use 3+ risk management platforms for specialized functions
GRC platform vendors increasingly partner with specialized risk tools rather than building native modules
objections.bestPractice: Map to GRC integration architecture; emphasize data federation, not consolidation

technologySystems.name: AML Transaction Monitoring System
technologySystems.description: Core transaction monitoring engine for suspicious activity detection including rule-based and behavioral analytics

technologySystems.name: Sanctions Screening Engine
technologySystems.description: Real-time and batch name screening against OFAC, UN, EU, HMT sanctions lists with fuzzy matching

technologySystems.name: KYC/CDD Platform
technologySystems.description: Customer onboarding, identity verification, beneficial ownership, and periodic review workflow management

technologySystems.name: Credit Risk Analytics Platform
technologySystems.description: PD/LGD/EAD modeling, CECL calculation, portfolio analytics, and capital allocation for credit risk

technologySystems.name: Market Risk Engine
technologySystems.description: VaR, stressed VaR, FRTB IMA/SA, sensitivity analysis, and backtesting for trading book risk

technologySystems.name: GRC Platform
technologySystems.description: Enterprise governance, risk, and compliance platform covering RCSA, loss events, issues, and controls

technologySystems.name: Fraud Detection and Prevention Platform
technologySystems.description: Real-time and batch fraud detection across payment channels with ML-based behavioral analytics

technologySystems.name: SOX Controls Automation Platform
technologySystems.description: Automated control testing, evidence collection, and deficiency tracking for SOX and internal controls

technologySystems.name: Consumer Compliance Monitoring System
technologySystems.description: UDAAP monitoring, fair lending analytics, complaint management, and product compliance review workflow

technologySystems.name: Cyber Risk Quantification Platform
technologySystems.description: FAIR-based or scenario-based cyber risk quantification with board reporting and insurance optimization

technologySystems.name: Third-Party Risk Management Platform
technologySystems.description: Vendor risk assessment, continuous monitoring, resilience scoring, and contract risk management

technologySystems.name: Regulatory Reporting Hub
technologySystems.description: Unified data pipeline for regulatory report generation, validation, and submission across multiple jurisdictions

technologySystems.name: Model Risk Management Platform
technologySystems.description: Model inventory, validation workflow, performance monitoring, and AI governance for enterprise model lifecycle

regulatoryFactors.name: Bank Secrecy Act / Anti-Money Laundering (BSA/AML)
regulatoryFactors.verticalSpecificNotes: False positive rate and alert disposition are direct examination focus areas. FinCEN beneficial ownership reporting (31 U.S.C. 5336) adds KYC complexity.

regulatoryFactors.name: Sarbanes-Oxley Act Section 404 (SOX ICFR)
complianceMaturityIndicators: No material weaknesses in last 2 years
External audit fees stable or declining
penaltyExamples: PCAOB inspection deficiencies: reputational and fee impact
regulatoryFactors.verticalSpecificNotes: Manual controls and ITGC deficiencies are top PCAOB inspection findings. Automation of management review controls is emerging priority.

regulatoryFactors.name: Basel Pillar 2 / ICAAP / Supervisory Review
complianceMaturityIndicators: ICAAP updated annually with stress testing linkage
Risk appetite statement reviewed within 12 months
penaltyExamples: Capital conservation buffer breaches lead to dividend restrictions
SREP score downgrade increases Pillar 2 add-on
regulatoryFactors.verticalSpecificNotes: ICAAP quality directly affects SREP score and Pillar 2 capital requirements. Climate risk increasingly integrated into Pillar 2 assessments.

regulatoryFactors.name: Federal Reserve SR 11-7 (Model Risk Management)
regulatoryFactors.verticalSpecificNotes: SR 11-7 applies to ALL models including AI/ML. OCC has elevated MRM to Level 3 priority. AI model governance is emerging examination focus.

regulatoryFactors.name: GDPR / Data Protection for Financial Services
regulatoryFactors.verticalSpecificNotes: AML/KYC data retention conflicts with GDPR data minimization create specific tension. Data lineage for regulatory reporting supports GDPR Article 30 compliance.

regulatoryFactors.name: CFPB Regulation Section 1033 (Consumer Access to Financial Records)
penaltyExamples: CFPB has not yet issued 1033 enforcement actions—expect 2025+
regulatoryFactors.verticalSpecificNotes: Section 1033 creates data governance complexity: firms must enable third-party data access while maintaining security and compliance controls.

regulatoryFactors.name: UK PRA PS21/18 (Operational Resilience)
complianceMaturityIndicators: Impact tolerances set for all critical services
penaltyExamples: Deutsche Bank operational resilience fine: GBP 4.3M (2023)
regulatoryFactors.verticalSpecificNotes: First major operational resilience regulation with hard deadline. Firms without service registers face remediation at scale.

regulatoryFactors.name: SEC Cybersecurity Disclosure Rules (2023)
penaltyExamples: SEC cybersecurity enforcement actions expected 2024-2025 as rules mature
regulatoryFactors.verticalSpecificNotes: Creates board-level accountability for cyber risk quantification and governance. Firms must have dollar-denominated materiality assessment capability.

regulatoryFactors.name: EU AI Act (High-Risk AI in Financial Services)
penaltyExamples: First enforcement actions expected 2025-2026 as Act becomes applicable
regulatoryFactors.verticalSpecificNotes: Credit scoring and fraud detection AI systems classified as high-risk. Direct overlap with existing MRM frameworks but adds EU-specific conformity requirements.

regulatoryFactors.name: OCC Heightened Standards (Guidelines Establishing Heightened Standards)
complianceMaturityIndicators: Risk governance framework aligned to 9 standards
penaltyExamples: Multiple OCC enforcement actions cite Heightened Standards gaps
regulatoryFactors.verticalSpecificNotes: Heightened Standards are the foundational OCC risk governance framework. Most large bank MRAs reference one or more of the 9 standards.

regulatoryFactors.name: ISSB S2 / TCFD Climate Disclosures
penaltyExamples: SEC climate disclosure rule stayed pending litigation; ISSB adoption accelerating in Asia-Pacific and EU
regulatoryFactors.verticalSpecificNotes: ISSB S2 is becoming the global baseline. US firms with international operations face multi-jurisdiction disclosure requirements. Data availability is primary gap.

competitorFactors.name: Tier 1 Bank AI-First AML Transformation
competitorFactors.description: JPMorgan, HSBC, and others deploying AI-powered transaction monitoring with 30-50% FP reduction and SAR quality improvement
evidence: JPMorgan COiN platform processes 12,000+ AML documents annually
HSBC AI TMS reduces alerts by 40%
competitorFactors.financialImplication: Competitive advantage in customer experience and investigator efficiency

competitorFactors.name: Digital-Only Bank KYC Speed Advantage
competitorFactors.description: Fintechs and digital banks achieving <5 minute KYC with biometric verification, setting customer expectation baseline
evidence: Revolut KYC: <3 minutes for 80% of customers
competitorFactors.financialImplication: Traditional bank abandonment rates increase 15-25% vs digital-first

competitorFactors.name: Neobank Fraud Prevention Real-Time Investment
competitorFactors.description: Monzo, Starling, and Chime investing in real-time ML fraud detection with APP scam reimbursement rates <10%
evidence: Monzo APP scam rate: <0.1% of transactions
Featurespace deployment at multiple UK banks
competitorFactors.financialImplication: Lower fraud losses and customer churn vs legacy batch systems

competitorFactors.name: European Bank FRTB Advanced Readiness
competitorFactors.description: Deutsche Bank, Barclays, and BNP Paribas completing FRTB IMA accreditation with capital optimization
evidence: Bank disclosures on FRTB parallel run results
competitorFactors.financialImplication: Capital efficiency advantage for IMA-approved firms vs standardized approach

competitorFactors.name: Big Tech Cyber Risk Quantification Adoption
competitorFactors.description: Microsoft, Amazon, and Google deploying FAIR-based cyber risk quantification for board reporting
evidence: SEC cyber disclosures with dollar impact ranges
competitorFactors.financialImplication: Board-level cyber investment decisions increasingly require dollar-denominated input

competitorFactors.name: Vendor Risk Tech Provider Market Consolidation
competitorFactors.description: BitSight acquiring multiple TPRM platforms; ServiceNow expanding GRC vendor risk modules
competitorFactors.financialImplication: Stand-alone point solutions face integration pressure from platform consolidation

competitorFactors.name: Climate Risk Integration in Credit Pricing
competitorFactors.description: European banks beginning to price transition risk into corporate lending spreads
evidence: ECB climate risk stress test results
Syndicated loan market ESG pricing differential emergence
competitorFactors.financialImplication: First-mover advantage in climate-adjusted pricing and portfolio steering

competitorFactors.name: RegTech Provider Automation Benchmarking
competitorFactors.description: AxiomSL, Regnology, and others publishing regulatory reporting automation benchmarks showing 40-60% cost reduction
evidence: AxiomSL case studies: 50% reporting headcount reduction
competitorFactors.financialImplication: Manual reporting operations face efficiency and talent retention disadvantage

workedExamples.title: Regional Bank Sanctions Screening Transformation
workedExamples.scenario: A $50B AUM regional bank with 3M annual wire transactions experienced a 96% sanctions false positive rate, consuming 25 FTE investigators and generating $2.4M annual labor cost. OFAC examination identified name-matching algorithm weaknesses.
calculationSteps: Current annual alert disposition cost: 3,000,000 * 0.96 * $12 (variable cost per alert) = $34.56M
Annual savings: $34.56M + $2.375M - $28.9M = $8.035M (using conservative alert variable cost)
Revised: FP reduction from 96% to 75% = 630,000 fewer false alerts * $75 average disposition cost = $47.25M annual
assumptions: Average false alert disposition cost: $75 (industry benchmark range $50-$120)
Headcount reduction proportional to FP reduction
workedExamples.keyTakeaway: Sanctions false positive reduction delivers the highest ROI in risk/compliance due to massive alert volume and labor intensity. The payback is typically under 3 months.

workedExamples.title: Credit Union Real-Time Fraud Detection on FedNow
workedExamples.scenario: A $12B credit union launched FedNow instant payments and experienced fraud losses of 22 bps on instant rails vs 4 bps on ACH. APP scams represented 60% of fraud losses. Regulatory reimbursement mandate expected within 18 months.
calculationSteps: Transaction hold friction reduction: 15,000 fewer holds * $12 friction cost = $180K
assumptions: APP scam share of fraud remains 60%
Customer complaint cost at $850 per complaint (industry benchmark)
Transaction hold friction cost at $12 per hold
workedExamples.keyTakeaway: Real-time fraud detection on instant payment rails pays for itself within 6 months when regulatory reimbursement mandates are included. The combined fraud loss + reimbursement liability + customer friction creates compelling ROI.

workedExamples.title: Global Bank Regulatory Reporting Automation
workedExamples.scenario: A top-20 global bank with operations in 35 jurisdictions spent $28M annually on regulatory reporting, employing 220 FTEs. First-pass filing accuracy was 82%, with 15% of filings requiring restatement or late submission. Data reconciliation between FFIEC, FINRA, and ECB reports consumed 8 days per cycle.
calculationSteps: Reconciliation time reduction: 8 days to 2 days = 6 days saved * 52 cycles * $5,000/day = $1.56M
Restatement avoidance: 0.15 * 200 filings * $40,000 avg restatement cost = $1.2M
Late filing penalty avoidance: 30 late filings * $25,000 avg penalty = $750K
First-pass accuracy improvement value: reduced manual rework = 200 filings * 0.18 * 40 hours * $85/hr = $122.4K
Efficiency reinvestment: 50% of FTE savings reinvested in analytics = $5.2M additional value
assumptions: FTE reduction achievable at 35% with automation (Protiviti benchmark: 30-45%)
Late filing penalty average $25K (range $5K-$1.5M for SEC)
Loaded FTE cost $135K for regulatory reporting specialists
50% of efficiency gains reinvested in higher-value analytics
workedExamples.keyTakeaway: Regulatory reporting automation delivers $10M+ annual savings for large banks while improving accuracy and reducing regulatory risk. The business case strengthens when restatement and penalty avoidance are quantified.

inheritedComponents: Value Driver Framework (5 categories: Cost Savings, Risk Reduction, Revenue Uplift, Working Capital, Compliance)
Base Persona Archetypes (PER001-PER015: CIO, CTO, CDO, COO, CFO, CRO, CCO, CISO, Internal Audit, Head of Trading, Head of Client, Head of Compliance, General Counsel, Chief Data Officer)

Evidence Source Types (regulatory_filing, financial_statement, technology_research, industry_association_survey, consulting_firm_report, proprietary_index, academic_research, news_media, government_data, technology_provider_benchmark)
Formula Templates (standardized NPV, payback, ROI calculation methodology)
Signal Source Taxonomy (technology_deployment, financial_metric, regulatory_filing, competitive_intelligence, market_data, news_media, executive_statement)
Benchmark Methodology (quantitative threshold validation, industry benchmark cross-reference, time-series trend analysis)
Governance Framework (version control, approval workflow, confidence calibration, source attribution, review cadence)
createdComponents: 36 vertical value driver mappings (RC-VD001 to RC-VD036)

overriddenComponents.overrideReason: Master pack CRO is generic. Subpack does not override but provides 6 MORE SPECIALIZED personas (AML Officer, Model Risk Manager, Regulatory Reporting Director, Fraud Prevention Director, Third-Party Risk Manager, Cyber Risk Lead) that add vertical granularity while inheriting PER003 for enterprise risk committee context.

Master pack CISO focuses on operational cybersecurity. Subpack adds Cyber Risk Lead (RC-PER006) who specializes in cyber RISK QUANTIFICATION and board reporting, distinct from CISO's operational security focus. Both personas coexist.
Master pack Head of Compliance is generic. Subpack adds AML Officer (RC-PER001) and Regulatory Reporting Director (RC-PER003) for specialized compliance sub-functions.

verticalPersonaAdditions: RC-PER001: BSA/AML Officer - specialized for AML program governance, sanctions, and SAR filing
RC-PER002: Chief Model Risk Officer / Model Risk Manager - specialized for SR 11-7 compliance, AI/ML governance
RC-PER003: Regulatory Reporting Director - specialized for end-to-end regulatory reporting operations
RC-PER004: Fraud Prevention Director - specialized for real-time fraud, APP scams, and insider threat
RC-PER005: Third-Party Risk Manager - specialized for vendor resilience and concentration risk
RC-PER006: Cyber Risk Lead - specialized for cyber risk quantification (FAIR) and board reporting
verticalKPIExtensions: RC-K001 to RC-K003: Sanctions screening metrics (false positive, latency, true positive detection)
RC-K004 to RC-K006: KYC/onboarding metrics (cycle time, periodic review coverage, EDD backlog)
RC-K007 to RC-K009: Credit risk model metrics (Gini, override rate, CECL volatility)
RC-K010 to RC-K012: Market risk metrics (VaR backtesting, FRTB multiplier, computation time)
RC-K013 to RC-K015: Operational risk metrics (loss capture, RCSA currency, scenario coverage)
RC-K016 to RC-K018: Fraud detection metrics (latency, APP scam false negative, instant payment loss rate)
RC-K019 to RC-K021: SOX metrics (testing hours, ITGC deficiency, access review completion)
RC-K022 to RC-K024: Consumer compliance metrics (complaint velocity, UDAAP risk, fair lending disparity)
